items.headline: Mexico becomes the first team into the World Cup knockout round
items.body: Mexico beat South Korea 1-0 in Guadalajara to win Group A and become the first nation through to the Round of 32. Luis Romo pounced on a goalkeeping spill in the 50th minute, and keeper Raul Rangel made a late double save to protect the clean sheet.
items.say: Did you catch Mexico? They're already the first team into the knockouts after that 1-0 over South Korea.

items.headline: Switzerland storm past 10-man Bosnia 4-1 in a wild finish
items.body: Substitute Johan Manzambi changed the game in Los Angeles with a 74th-minute volley and a second in the 90th. Ruben Vargas struck after Bosnia went down to 10 men, and captain Granit Xhaka buried a penalty with the final kick.
items.say: Switzerland put four past Bosnia — wild ending, a red card and a penalty with the literal last kick.

items.headline: Knicks finally parade up the Canyon of Heroes — first title since 1973
items.body: An estimated two million fans flooded Lower Manhattan as the Knicks rode floats up Broadway to celebrate their first championship in 53 years. Finals MVP Jalen Brunson soaked in 'M-V-P' chants, and Alicia Keys sang 'Empire State of Mind' at City Hall.
items.say: The Knicks parade happened — first one in 53 years. Two million people in the streets.

items.headline: IEM Cologne Major playoffs open with a 9z upset scare on FURIA
items.body: The CS2 Cologne Major playoffs kicked off in front of a packed Lanxess Arena. Underdogs 9z stunned FURIA by taking the opening map before the Brazilians clawed back to win the quarterfinal 2-1.
items.say: The Counter-Strike Cologne Major playoffs started — 9z actually took a map off FURIA before bowing out.

items.headline: Wyndham Clark torches a foggy Shinnecock with a 64
items.body: After a two-hour morning fog delay, Wyndham Clark posted a 6-under 64 — one of the lowest rounds in US Open history at Shinnecock — to seize a big lead, while Jon Rahm and Rory McIlroy lurked behind.
items.say: Wyndham Clark opened the US Open with a 64 at Shinnecock after a fog delay — basically lapped the field on day one.

items.headline: Hurricanes are the new Stanley Cup champs after 20-year wait
items.body: Carolina is still basking in its second Stanley Cup, sealed with a 3-0 Game 6 shutout of Vegas — their first title since 2006. Jordan Staal won the Conn Smythe as the oldest recipient ever, and Rod Brind'Amour now has one as coach.
items.say: The Hurricanes are champs again — first time since 2006, and the same guy who captained that team coached this one.

items.headline: Toy Story 5 opens tomorrow — and tracking says it's huge
items.body: Pixar's fifth Woody-and-Buzz adventure hits theaters Friday with strong reviews and a projected $160M opening — what would be the biggest debut of 2026. This time the toys face Lilypad, Bonnie's addictive kiddie tablet.
items.say: Toy Story 5 opens tomorrow — reviews are great and they're projecting the biggest opening of the year.

items.headline: Taylor Swift wrote a new song for Toy Story 5 — and it's a hit
items.body: Swift's end-credits ballad for Toy Story 5, written with Jack Antonoff, is inspired by Jessie and has already smashed first-day streaming records as it climbs the Hot 100.
items.say: Taylor Swift wrote a song for Toy Story 5 about Jessie — it's already breaking streaming records.

items.headline: Netflix drops Harlan Coben's 'I Will Find You' today
items.body: The eight-episode thriller lands on Netflix Thursday, starring Sam Worthington as a father wrongly imprisoned for his son's murder who sets out to find the boy after learning he may still be alive.
items.say: New Harlan Coben thriller dropped on Netflix today — 'I Will Find You,' with Sam Worthington. Looks like a binge.
